steps.title: Identify the central paradox
steps.content: Quito has much higher park usage than Fortaleza (82.9% vs. 35.7%), yet residents of Quito have lower access to amenities near parks. This paradox proves the usage difference cannot stem from better nearby amenities in Quito.

steps.title: Match the finding to the choices
steps.content: Choice B correctly states both the observation—Quito residents use parks more—and the conclusion the passage draws: that difference is not due to amenity access near parks. The other three either introduce factors not in the study (choice A expands to overall amenities, choice C cites park prevalence) or speculate beyond the passage's scope (choice D). That matches choice B — B is correct.
